version.approval_notes: OpenPath is a managed education allowlist system. This Firefox extension is the browser companion for managed OpenPath devices. Permission rationale: <all_urls> lets the page activity and page-resource observers detect blocked third-party resources regardless of origin; webRequest/webRequestBlocking detect and apply managed blocked navigation/resource decisions; webNavigation clears per-tab state when navigation changes; tabs scopes badge and popup data to the active tab; nativeMessaging talks only to the local OpenPath native host installed on the same managed computer; storage keeps managed config and local runtime state in browser storage; clipboardWrite copies blocked-domain lists only after a user action. The auto-allow flow observes fetch, XMLHttpRequest, and dynamic resource insertions and sends only candidate host/URL, origin page, resource type, and policy reason to the configured OpenPath service. Firefox loads the MAIN-world page-resource observer via manifest, and the page-activity content script can inject the same observer as a page script for Firefox-version coverage. It does not include third-party analytics, profiling, advertising, or sale/sharing of browser data. The Google games guard is limited to supported Google Search and Doodles domains and applies a school policy to Google Search/Doodles games. Human-readable source is attached with the submission; see SOURCE_REVIEW_NOTES.md in that archive for rebuild and review notes.
